title: Possible explanation for Asukas mom
wikitext:
[]: Possible explanation for Asuka's mom?             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 04, 2014 4:47 pm Came across this interesting disorder the other day called Capgras , and thought it actually might help explain what went on with Asuka's mom. Perhaps give more of a window into what that whole mess must have been like for Asuka and/or her dad. Essentially this condition breaks down the emotional recognition of people, often just a specific person, such that while the sufferer knows who the person seems to be they feel something isn't right about them. Thus the afflicted tends to eventually believe that this person is an "impostor" or "double" that is trying to take their loved one's place, and may or may not project what they believe is the "real" person onto another person, object, or hallucination.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 04, 2014 5:38 pm So loss of half of the soul would give a person Capgras? I can see how that would work, except for the fact that Capgras is purely inside the head, and loss of the soul is a very fictional and ethereal concept. While I'm not sure how applicable it is to the in-show universe, I DO think Capgras is a great way of describing what happened to Kyoko in real world terms.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 04, 2014 5:46 pm Well yeah, I'm not saying that's necessarily her actual diagnosis... just that it might give insight into just what the hell was going on there. Possibly Capgras was the inspiration for that scenario.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 04, 2014 5:58 pm A lot of weird and disturbing things can happen if some part of the brain breaks, and some do not... Like this disease where all you speak an write is gibberish, and you understand other people as gibberish, but you can repeat and understand song lyrics perfectly fine - because they're processed in the other hemisphere. Ultimately, what is the human soul but a computer program that run on the hardware of our brain? That's obviously not necessarily true in NGE verse but... that's what it's all a metaphor for, or they're two ways to describe the same thing. In any case, that's an idea for where to get inspiration/ore of a feel for whatever Kyoko's perception and the feelings/reactions of the people were like, or if you want technobabble for it, it needn't be completely accurate for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Feb 04, 2014 6:28 pm Bodies are just meat sacks for souls and Kyoko lost the part of hers that recognized her daughter. I have no doubt it's inspired by this disorder, though. It's a heartbreaking and sometimes terrifying and horribly frustrating thing to deal with, trust me.
